stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates continued community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's Llama models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in generative AI tasks.
The model is categorized under key ML tags like 'transformers' and 'llama', signaling its relevance in the open-source AI community.

stories.title: MetaBackdoor: Exploiting Positional Encoding as a Backdoor Attack Surface in LLMs
stories.author: Ahmed Salem
bullet_points: Introduces 'MetaBackdoor,' a novel class of backdoor attack against Large Language Models (LLMs).
This attack exploits positional encoding within LLMs, moving beyond traditional content-based triggers.
Highlights a significant security vulnerability in LLMs, impacting safety- and privacy-critical applications.

stories.title: linux 0-day, access root-owned files as an unprivileged user
stories.author: github.com via dzwdz
bullet_points: A critical Linux 0-day vulnerability has been discovered.
The exploit allows an unprivileged user to gain access to root-owned files.
This represents a significant security risk to Linux systems.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using both local and cloud models.
A key feature is the streamlined setup, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.
The partnership involves Ollama, OpenAI, and ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools).

stories.title: YouTube is expanding its AI deepfake detection tool to all adult users
stories.author: Mia Sato
bullet_points: YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection tool to all adult users (18+).
The feature uses a selfie-style scan to monitor the platform for potential deepfakes of the user.
If a match is found, YouTube will alert the user.

stories.title: ArXiv will ban researchers who upload papers full of AI slop
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: ArXiv is implementing a policy to combat the submission of low-quality research papers containing AI-generated content ('AI slop').
The ban targets authors whose papers show 'incontrovertible evidence' of unchecked LLM generation, such as hallucinations or LLM meta-comments.
This move signals a growing institutional effort within academic publishing to maintain research integrity in the age of generative AI.

stories.title: OpenAI keeps shuffling its executives in bid to win AI agent battle
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: OpenAI is undergoing another executive reorganization to focus efforts.
The company is consolidating products to prioritize and 'go all-in' on AI agents.
Greg Brockman has been designated the official lead for all product-related initiatives at OpenAI.

stories.title: Google updates its spam rules to include attempts to ‘manipulate’ AI
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google has updated its spam policies to specifically target attempts to 'manipulate' its AI models.
This policy update applies to content appearing in new features like AI Overview and AI Mode within Google Search.
The change reinforces Google's stance against deceptive techniques designed to unfairly influence search results.

stories.title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement is getting messy as judge delays approval
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic is involved in a major copyright settlement valued at $1.5 billion.
The settlement's approval is facing delays from the presiding judge.
Legal disputes have arisen, with accusations that lawyers were rushing the deal to secure substantial fees.

stories.title: OpenAI feels “burned” by Apple’s crappy ChatGPT integration, insiders say
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The story involves a legal dispute where a judge ordered Apple to release internal messages.
The dispute centers on a secretive deal between Apple and OpenAI regarding ChatGPT integration.
OpenAI reportedly feels negatively impacted or 'burned' by Apple's implementation of ChatGPT.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework to update LLMs without retraining core parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being static after pretraining, allowing for timely, domain-specific knowledge integration.
Encodes new knowledge into a dedicated memory model, offering an efficient alternative to full model fine-tuning.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework to update LLMs with new, domain-specific knowledge.
MeMo achieves this by encoding new knowledge into a dedicated memory model without altering the core LLM parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being 'frozen' after pretraining, making them more suitable for real-world, timely applications.

stories.title: Forgetting That Sticks: Quantization-Permanent Unlearning via Circuit Attribution
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in AI safety: the failure of standard unlearning evaluations because deployed models are quantized.
Identifies a 'systematic dual failure' where gradient-based unlearning methods lose effectiveness under 4-bit quantization.
Proposes 'Quantization-Permanent Unlearning' to address the instability of forgetting when models are compressed.

stories.title: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
stories.author: Alexey A. Shvets
bullet_points: Introduces a novel, reusable framework to audit the collective coverage of LLM attack benchmarks.
The framework uses a 4x6 Target x Technique matrix, grounded in STRIDE, derived from a 507-leaf taxonomy.
The audit is based on analyzing 932 arXiv security studies from 2023-2026 to validate threat surface coverage.

stories.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is now generally available on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform
stories.author: gemini-enterprise
bullet_points: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite has been released and is now generally available.
The model is positioned as the fastest and most cost-efficient option.
It is available on the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content references the 'bigscience/bloom' model, a significant large language model.
It is presented within the Hugging Face Benchmarks context, indicating a focus on model performance and comparison.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation', highlighting the model's primary application.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM platforms and proprietary APIs.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
Users can leverage various open-weight alternatives like gpt-oss:20b or gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: AI radio hosts demonstrate why AI can’t be trusted alone
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Andon Labs is conducting experiments where AI agents autonomously run businesses, including radio stations.
The experiment features four popular AI models—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, and Grok—each managing a distinct radio station.
The article suggests the demonstration highlights limitations or untrustworthiness when AI operates without human oversight.

stories.title: Three's a party: US, China, and now Russia are on the prowl in GEO
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: US, China, and Russia are increasing their surveillance activities in Geosynchronous Orbit (GEO).
The article suggests that satellites are not as stealthy as previously assumed, making them visible against space's blackness.
The focus is on the growing geopolitical competition and monitoring capabilities in space.

stories.title: RefDecoder: Enhancing Visual Generation with Conditional Video Decoding
stories.author: Ranjay Krishna
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in latent diffusion models where decoders are often unconditional despite heavily conditioned denoising networks.
Proposes RefDecoder, an architecture that applies equal conditioning to the decoder to improve visual generation.
Aims to reduce loss of detail and inconsistency in video generation by preserving structural integrity.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world environments.
The core innovation is replaying actual, chronological world events (like news articles) to provide realistic training data.
The system allows agents to forecast future events beyond their existing knowledge cutoff while interacting with the historical timeline.

stories.title: When Are Two Networks the Same? Tensor Similarity for Mechanistic Interpretability
stories.author: Thomas Dooms
bullet_points: Introduces 'tensor similarity,' a new weight-based metric for tensor-based models.
Addresses limitations of existing similarity measures by being invariant to weight-space symmetries.
Serves as a crucial tool for mechanistic interpretability, allowing researchers to verify if different model parts implement the same computation.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
Identifies gradient conflict and optimization instability when co-training disparate PDE regimes (e.g., fluid dynamics vs. porous media flows).
Proposes a solution involving sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing to manage incompatible spectral and geometric demands.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening in medical imaging.
Addresses limitations in current models, specifically poor interpretability and lack of historical case referencing.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to improve transparency and performance in clinical settings.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (precise timing).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to bridge the gap between semantic richness and temporal accuracy.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: The paper critiques current AI governance frameworks for demanding safety claims that behavioral assurance cannot verify.
Key required properties, such as absence of hidden objectives and bounded catastrophic capability, are difficult to prove.
Current assurance methods (behavioral evaluations and red-teaming) are limited by what is observable in model outputs.

stories.title: Hand-in-the-Loop: Improving Dexterous VLA via Seamless Interventional Correction
stories.author: Ruoshi Wen
bullet_points: The paper addresses compounding errors in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models when performing dexterous manipulation.
It tackles the challenge of applying Interactive Imitation Learning (IIL) to high-DoF robotic hands.
A key hurdle identified is the command mismatch during human takeover, leading to abrupt policy execution failures.

stories.title: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Yongliang Shen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of coarse, trajectory-level reward signals in Reinforcement Learning (RL) for LLM agents.
It introduces On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) to provide dense, token-level guidance using a teacher branch with privileged context.
A key challenge identified is the instability of transferring OPSD to multi-turn agents due to compounding supervision issues.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework for testing AI agents in dynamic environments.
The core innovation is replaying real-world events chronologically to provide realistic testing scenarios.
Allows agents to forecast future events beyond their training data cutoff while interacting with historical context.

stories.title: Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: The paper investigates how different retrieval strategies impact agentic search systems powered by LLMs.
It addresses a gap in current literature by systematically comparing retrieval choice against agent architecture and tool-calling methods.
The research focuses on complex LLM agent workflows that autonomously retrieve information and use tools to complete tasks.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of temporal ambiguity in unstructured clinical narratives.
It proposes a multimodal alignment approach (Retrieval-Augmented) to combine rich text context with precise structured EHR data.
The application focuses on reconstructing accurate clinical timelines, which is critical for modeling patient trajectories in complex conditions like sepsis.

stories.title: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Yongliang Shen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of coarse, trajectory-level reward signals in Reinforcement Learning (RL) for LLM agents.
It introduces On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) to provide dense, token-level guidance using a teacher branch with privileged context.
A key challenge identified is the instability of transferring OPSD to multi-turn agents due to compounding supervision issues.

stories.title: MemEye: A Visual-Centric Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Agent Memory
stories.author: Ruixiang Tang
bullet_points: Introduces MemEye, a new evaluation framework for multimodal agent memory.
Addresses the gap where existing evaluations often rely only on textual traces, ignoring fine-grained visual evidence.
Focuses on testing agents' ability to preserve visual evidence necessary for complex, state-changing reasoning.

stories.title: Building "Sweets Vault" - a multimodal Gemini Agent with physical hardware integration
stories.author: rsamborski
bullet_points: The project describes 'Sweets Vault,' a multimodal Gemini Agent.
It features integration with physical hardware, suggesting real-world application.
The stated goal is to motivate young children (seven-year-olds) for educational tasks like reading and handwriting.

stories.title: Bigger AI models aren't always better. Here's how to actually choose.
stories.author: rohini_gaonkar
bullet_points: Challenges the assumption that larger AI models inherently perform better.
Focuses on providing practical guidance for selecting appropriate AI models.
Highlights the issue of AI model reliability, referencing hallucination.
